input_row.title: Ensure clusters are created with Private Nodes
input_row.description: Disable public IP addresses for cluster nodes, so that they only have private IP addresses. Private Nodes are nodes with no public IP addresses.
input_row.rationale: Disabling public IP addresses on cluster nodes restricts access to only internal networks, forcing attackers to obtain local network access before attempting to compromise the underlying Kubernetes hosts. Impact: To enable Private Nodes, the cluster has to also be configured with a private master IP range and IP Aliasing enabled. Private Nodes do not have outbound access to the public internet. If you want to provide outbound Internet access for your private nodes, you can use Cloud NAT or you can manage your own NAT gateway.
input_row.audit: Check for the following to be 'enabled: true' export CLUSTER_NAME=<your cluster name> export RESOURCE_GROUP=<your resource group name> az aks show --name ${CLUSTER_NAME} --resource-group ${RESOURCE_GROUP} --query "apiServerAccessProfile.enablePrivateCluster" Check for the following is not null: az aks show --name ${CLUSTER_NAME} --resource-group ${RESOURCE_GROUP} --query "apiServerAccessProfile.authorizedIpRanges"
input_row.remediation: az aks create \ --resource-group <private-cluster-resource-group> \ --name <private-cluster-name> \ --load-balancer-sku standard \ --enable-private-cluster \ --network-plugin azure \ --vnet-subnet-id <subnet-id> \ --docker-bridge-address \ --dns-service-ip \ --service-cidr Where --enable-private-cluster is a mandatory flag for a private cluster. References: 1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/private-clusters
input_row.impact: To enable Private Nodes, the cluster has to also be configured with a private master IP range and IP Aliasing enabled. Private Nodes do not have outbound access to the public internet. If you want to provide outbound Internet access for your private nodes, you can use Cloud NAT or you can manage your own NAT gateway.

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
- The audit field specifies Azure CLI commands to check for compliance.
- The control examines properties related to the configuration of AKS clusters, which can be queried through Azure Resource Manager APIs.
- The presence of the API/CLI commands in the audit field supports an automated approach as it indicates that the properties can be directly validated using Azure's tools.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_aks_private_nodes_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
The automated program would check:
- Use Azure CLI commands to validate cluster configurations in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS).
- Enumerate resources within specified subscriptions and resource groups for AKS clusters.

The exact checks to execute include:
1. **Check if Private Cluster is enabled**:
   Command: 
   ```bash
   az aks show --name ${CLUSTER_NAME} --resource-group ${RESOURCE_GROUP} --query "apiServerAccessProfile.enablePrivateCluster"
   ```
   - **Pass Criteria**: The output must be `true`.

2. **Check if authorized IP ranges are set**:
   Command: 
   ```bash
   az aks show --name ${CLUSTER_NAME} --resource-group ${RESOURCE_GROUP} --query "apiServerAccessProfile.authorizedIpRanges"
   ```
   - **Pass Criteria**: The output must not be `null`.

These program checks will help ensure that AKS clusters are configured with private nodes, reinforcing security by preventing public access and confirming compliance with the control requirements.
